page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton2 processor. It features 48 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with a 1:1 vCPU-to-physical-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a compute-optimized allocation of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance.

In benchmark testing, the c6gn.12xlarge exhibits strong multi-core capabilities, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 72,814.16 and high static web serving throughput of 40.09 GB/sec. It also excels in Redis SET operations, reaching 58.46 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is a relative bottleneck; the single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores fall into the lower percentiles. Large-block memory write bandwidth is measured at 75.13 GB/sec, while read bandwidth reaches 167.25 GB/sec. Large LLM inference workloads, such as text generation on a 70B model, perform poorly at 2.78 tokens per second due to the lack of hardware accelerators.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-intensive and EBS-reliant operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a high density of dedicated physical cores and substantial network throughput without the premium associated with x86 licensing or specialized GPU hardware. This profile makes the c6gn.12xlarge highly suitable for multi-threaded compute workloads, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching layers, and network-bound distributed applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 96.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core), a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 72,814.16 and static web serving throughput of 40.09 GB/sec. While single-core performance and large LLM inference tasks show relative weakness, its high-throughput networking and dedicated core density offer qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, high-volume web serving, Redis caching, and network-intensive compute tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 96 GB of memory, and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated ARM64 compute platform delivering high-throughput networking and strong multi-core efficiency for parallelized network-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and networking
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput static web serving
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for Redis caching, web serving, and parallelized compute tasks
